question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getCommon()] Can an unprivileged external user set hiero.mirror.importer.downloader.pathRefreshInterval to a very large Duration via environment variable injection, causing the node path cache to become stale and the downloader to continue using outdated node account IDs or node IDs for path construction, potentially fetching record files from wrong or attacker-controlled paths that contain transactions without proper signer authorization?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: isEnabled()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that enabled=true is the default value in RecordDownloaderProperties (with no @NotNull or other constraint requiring explicit configuration) to ensure that in a misconfigured deployment where the property is not explicitly set, the downloader automatically starts processing transaction records from the cloud bucket without any operator having explicitly authorized the ingestion of those records?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getStreamType()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that StreamType.RECORD has a fileCloseInterval of 2 seconds and the frequency is 500ms (faster than the close interval) to cause the downloader to attempt to process record files that are still being written by nodes, potentially reading incomplete files with partial signatures that pass hash verification but lack full signer approval?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: setWriteFiles(boolean)] Can an unprivileged external user enable writeFiles=true via environment variable injection, causing the archive path in importerProperties.getArchiveDestinationFolderPath() to be used for writing verified record files, and then exploit a symlink attack on the archive destination directory to redirect the written files to a location that is later read back as a LOCAL source, creating a feedback loop where attacker-modified transaction records are re-ingested as if they were legitimately signed?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getCommon()] Can an unprivileged external user set hiero.mirror.importer.downloader.cloudProvider=LOCAL via environment variable injection, causing the downloader to read record files from the local filesystem instead of a cloud bucket, and then exploit a file write vulnerability elsewhere in the system to place forged record files in the expected local path, which are then processed without the cloud-based signature verification that would normally require node owner approval?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getCommon()] Can an unprivileged external user set hiero.mirror.importer.downloader.accessKey and hiero.mirror.importer.downloader.secretKey to credentials for an attacker-controlled cloud account via environment variable injection, causing the downloader to authenticate to a malicious S3 bucket that serves forged record files with fabricated node signatures, bypassing the requirement for legitimate transaction signer approval?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getFrequency()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a @DurationMin constraint on the frequency field in RecordDownloaderProperties (unlike CommonDownloaderProperties.pathRefreshInterval which has @DurationMin(seconds=1)) to set frequency to Duration.ZERO or a negative duration, causing undefined behavior in the @Scheduled(fixedDelayString) expression and potentially causing the scheduler to run continuously without delay, processing transaction records before signature verification completes?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getCommon()] Can an unprivileged external user set hiero.mirror.importer.downloader.consensusRatio to the minimum allowed value of 0 (permitted by @Min(0)) via environment variable injection, causing ConsensusValidatorImpl.validate() to enter the early-return branch when signatureHashMap is non-empty, effectively accepting any single verified signature as sufficient consensus to authorize transaction records without requiring approval from the minimum required fraction of node owners?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getCommon()] Can an unprivileged external user set hiero.mirror.importer.downloader.downloadRatio to exactly equal consensusRatio via environment variable injection, causing partialCollection() to select only the minimum number of nodes needed for consensus, and then perform a targeted man-in-the-middle attack on those specific nodes' cloud storage paths to substitute forged signature files, authorizing transactions without approval from the full set of legitimate node owners?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: isWriteSignatures()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the default writeSignatures=false setting to ensure that when a malicious record file is accepted (due to a compromised node key meeting the consensusRatio), no signature files are archived, eliminating the forensic evidence needed to detect that the transaction was authorized without legitimate owner approval, making the attack undetectable after the fact?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getCommon()] Can an unprivileged external user set hiero.mirror.importer.downloader.batchSize=1 via environment variable injection, causing the downloader to process only one signature file per node per batch, and then exploit the sequential processing in verifySigsAndDownloadDataFiles() to ensure that only a single node's signature is evaluated per cycle, potentially allowing a transaction record signed by only one node to be accepted if the consensusRatio is also manipulated?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: setEnabled(boolean)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the publicly-accessible setEnabled() method (generated by @Data and exposed via the DownloaderProperties interface) through a deserialization gadget or reflection-based attack in a misconfigured Spring application context to set enabled=true on a previously-disabled RecordDownloaderProperties bean, causing the record downloader to resume processing transaction records that were halted due to a detected signer compromise?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getCommon()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that CommonDownloaderProperties.isAnonymousCredentials() returns true for known networks (mainnet, testnet) by default, causing the downloader to use anonymous access to the public Hedera bucket, and then perform a DNS spoofing or BGP hijacking attack to redirect the S3 endpoint to an attacker-controlled server that serves forged record files with fabricated node signatures?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getStreamType()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that StreamType.RECORD uses the file extension .rcd and the downloader processes files matching this extension, to upload a crafted .rcd file to the cloud bucket (if write access is obtained) that contains transaction records with manipulated signer fields, which are then downloaded and processed by the RecordFileDownloader without verifying that the transactions were approved by the actual account owners?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getCommon()] Can an unprivileged external user set hiero.mirror.importer.downloader.sources.connectionTimeout to an extremely small value via environment variable injection, causing connection timeouts for all legitimate node sources, and then provide a fast-responding attacker-controlled source as a fallback, which serves forged record files with fabricated signatures that authorize transactions without legitimate owner approval?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getCommon()] Can an unprivileged external user set hiero.mirror.importer.downloader.sources.backoff to Duration.ZERO via environment variable injection, causing the downloader to immediately retry a failed source without any backoff, and then exploit a timing attack where a legitimate source is temporarily unavailable while an attacker-controlled source is available, causing the downloader to accept forged transaction records from the malicious source?
